title: sedum alpestre subsp. erythraeum
intro: Sedum alpestre subsp. erythraeum, also known as mountain stonecrop, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Crassulaceae. This succulent plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Sedum alpestre subsp. erythraeum include its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as its ability to act as an antioxidant, protecting against cell damage and oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of this plant are often made by infusing the leaves in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves that can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
